data.title: As the DSO workflow, I can defer an out-of-scope PR comment by creating a tracking ticket and replying with the ticket reference
data.description:
## Description

**What**: Orchestrator logic for the defer action handler: creates a DSO tracking ticket for the deferred comment, writes the ticket ID to the normalized JSON file, and posts a reply to the comment thread with the ticket ID and a brief rationale.
**Why**: Out-of-scope or ambiguous comments still deserve a response. Creating a tracking ticket ensures engineer feedback isn't silently dropped while communicating that the workflow has intentionally deferred action on this comment.
**Scope**:
- IN: Receive a pre-classified defer comment; create a DSO tracking ticket via .claude/scripts/dso ticket create using the comment body as description context; write the ticket ID to the normalized JSON file BEFORE any GitHub API reply call; use the shared reply-posting function from Story 1 to post a thread reply with the ticket ID and brief rationale; retry idempotency: if ticket ID is already present in the normalized JSON (non-null), skip ticket creation and proceed directly to reply posting; all reply bodies begin with <!-- dso-agent-reply -->
- OUT: Comment classification (Story 5); tickets for comments classified as accept or defend; action on the defer comment itself (it remains an open PR comment — the ticket represents a follow-up)

## Done Definitions

- When this story is complete, a defer-classified comment results in a new DSO tracking ticket being created with the comment body as context
  ← Satisfies: "creates a tracking ticket" (SC4)
- When this story is complete, the ticket ID is written to the normalized JSON file before any GitHub API reply is posted
  ← Satisfies: "writes the ticket ID to the normalized JSON before any API reply call" (SC4)
- When this story is complete, the reply posted to the comment thread includes the ticket ID, a brief deferral rationale, and begins with <!-- dso-agent-reply -->
  ← Satisfies: "posts a reply with the ticket ID and brief rationale; reply body begins with <!-- dso-agent-reply -->" (SC4)
- When this story is complete, if the ticket ID is already present in the normalized JSON (retry case), ticket creation is skipped and only the reply is posted
  ← Satisfies: "Defer creates duplicate tickets on retry (medium, runtime)" prevention (epic Scenario Analysis)
- When this story is complete, unit tests are written and passing for all new or modified logic
  ← TDD Done-of-Done requirement

## Considerations

- [Reliability] Ticket ID must be persisted to JSON before reply call — if reply fails and handler retries, non-null ticket ID in JSON must skip ticket creation; test must confirm no duplicate ticket is created on retry
- [Testing] Test must confirm that if ticket creation fails, no reply is posted and the handler exits with an error (fail-safe: don't reply without a ticket to reference)
- [Maintainability] Use the shared thread-reply posting function from Story 1 for the reply call — do not reimplement is_inline endpoint routing here

## Escalation Policy

**Escalation policy**: Proceed unless a significant assumption is required to continue — one that could send the implementation in the wrong direction. Escalate only when genuinely blocked without a reasonable inference. Document all assumptions made without escalating.
